comments.author: asx8678
comments.text: Synthesis happening implicitly via the writing process. Key decisions locked in: (a) /Owl umbrella with ///optional  from plan_v0.3; (b) Universal Agent Runtime with profile hot-swap (planner/coder/reviewer/critic/tester/repair/json) — Code Puppy's BaseAgent pattern adapted to long-lived OTP process; (c) durable workflow journal from upgrades/12_durable_execution.md as the runtime backbone; (d) provider behaviour from upgrades/11_provider_contract.md with bidirectional tool-call map; (e) hook engine ported conceptually from Code Puppy hook_engine/ with priority/exit-code semantics; (f) renamed memory tiers: Event Log (Bronze→), Session Memory, Task Memory, Finding Store (Silver→), Persistent Memory + Project Lessons (Gold→), Artifact Store, Repair Queue; (g) Nano Repair Mode as a dedicated subsystem with fallback minimal-repair binary; (h) SSH remote workers post-MVP. Will mark this done when both deliverables are written.

comments.author: pack-leader-0485da
comments.text:
SYNTHESIS PART 1/3 — Investigation plan and research synthesis for tet-d1c

Investigation plan:
- Scope: research/planning only; no source code, patches, or final deliverable files.
- Use Phase 2 extraction as baseline, then spot-check actual local source paths.
- Decision filters: Code Puppy for agent/session/model/tool behavior; Elixir/OTP for supervision/runtime/state recovery; Swarm-style task/hooks for deterministic discipline; Codex-style policy for shell/approval safety; CLI core wins over web; Phoenix optional adapter only; use renamed memory terms only (Event Log, Session Memory, Task Memory, Finding Store, Persistent Memory, Artifact Store, Repair Queue, Project Lessons, Error Log, checkpoints).

Sources spot-checked / citation anchors:
- Meta constraints: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md
- Prior plans: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/tet agent_platform_minimal_core.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/FINAL_ULTIMATE_PLAN.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/upgrades/11_provider_contract.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/upgrades/12_durable_execution.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/extensions/15_mcp_and_agents.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan 3/plan/docs/01_architecture.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan 3/plan/docs/05_tools_policy_approvals_verification.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/first big plan/agent_platform_plan_final.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/swarm-sdk-architecture.pdf
- Code Puppy anchors: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/agents/base_agent.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/agents/_builder.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/agents/_runtime.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/agents/agent_manager.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/model_factory.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/round_robin_model.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/models.json; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/tools/__init__.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/mcp_/manager.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/hook_engine/engine.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/session_storage.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/messaging/bus.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/config.py
- Codex / adjacent anchors: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/core/src/exec_policy.rs; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/dispatcher.rs; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/exec-server/README.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/state/migrations/0006_memories.sql; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/jido-main/README.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/llxprt-code-main/README.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/llxprt-code-main/docs/shell-replacement.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/oh-my-pi-main/README.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/pi-mono-main/README.md

Research synthesis table:
1. prompt.md -> hard constraints: CLI-first, Phoenix optional, Universal Agent Runtime, Nano Repair Mode, renamed memory terms -> map to standalone tet_cli/tet_runtime with optional web adapter and explicit repair subsystem -> cite /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md.
2. tet agent_platform_minimal_core.md -> smallest safe core and allowlisted verification -> map to lean OTP supervision + durable Event Log before advanced orchestration -> cite /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/tet agent_platform_minimal_core.md.
3. plan_v0.3 and plan 3 architecture -> runtime must be owned by core/CLI, not Phoenix -> map to umbrella-style split: domain, runtime, CLI, store adapters, optional Phoenix -> cite /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/FINAL_ULTIMATE_PLAN.md and /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan 3/plan/docs/01_architecture.md.
4. Code Puppy agent runtime -> BaseAgent/builder/runtime/history/compaction and agent manager patterns -> map to Universal Agent Runtime GenServer owning profile, history, attachments, tools, model config, task state, and cache hints -> cite base_agent.py, _builder.py, _runtime.py, agent_manager.py.
5. Code Puppy model layer -> model registry, provider-specific settings, round-robin, model pins, large-context hints -> map to ModelRouter -> ProviderAdapter -> StructuredOutputLayer with per-profile pins, streaming, retries, fallback, telemetry -> cite model_factory.py, round_robin_model.py, models.json, upgrades/11_provider_contract.md.
6. Code Puppy tools/MCP -> broad tool registry and MCP manager lifecycle -> map to typed Tet.Tool contracts, namespaced MCP tools, operator read-only allowlist, policy gates before execution -> cite tools/__init__.py, mcp_/manager.py, extensions/15_mcp_and_agents.md.
7. Code Puppy persistence/messaging/config -> pickle sessions, metadata, UI message bus, XDG/legacy config -> map to durable store adapters, append-only Event Log, Session Memory, Artifact Store, and runtime event subscriptions for CLI/optional Phoenix -> cite session_storage.py, messaging/bus.py, config.py.
8. Swarm / first big plan -> active-task requirement, category gates, priority hooks, guidance after task ops, complete event capture -> map to TaskManager + HookManager GenServers and five invariants in both final plans -> cite swarm-sdk-architecture.pdf and first big plan.
9. Codex CLI -> shell/exec policy, approvals, hook summaries, exec-server lifecycle -> map to allowlisted verifier commands first, shell approvals, remote worker heartbeat/cancel/output streaming -> cite exec_policy.rs, dispatcher.rs, exec-server/README.md, 0006_memories.sql.
10. Jido / LLxprt / Oh My Pi / Pi Mono -> optional framework ideas, safe shell substitution, CLI DX, SSH/theming/plugin/packaging inspiration -> map to later-phase optional adoption and DX, not core authority -> cite jido README, llxprt README and shell-replacement.md, oh-my-pi README, pi-mono README.

Missing-source handling: G-domain / agent-domain and exact LLExpert sources were not verified locally; do not invent paths or facts. Use explicit prompt requirements plus verified adjacent sources only.

comments.author: pack-leader-0485da
comments.text:
SYNTHESIS PART 2/3 — ADR-style decisions for tet-d1c

ADR-001 CLI-first core and optional Phoenix boundary: ACCEPTED. Build a standalone Elixir/OTP CLI-first runtime. Phoenix LiveView is optional adapter/control surface only and must not own session state, tools, policies, storage, providers, approvals, verification, Event Log, or repair. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/FINAL_ULTIMATE_PLAN.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan 3/plan/docs/01_architecture.md.

ADR-002 OTP topology and Jido-vs-custom: ACCEPTED. Use custom OTP runtime first. Keep pure domain/contracts, runtime OTP supervision, CLI adapter, store adapters, and optional Phoenix adapter separated. Jido is design inspiration / possible later backend only if measured runtime pain justifies it and it stays behind the same runtime contract. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan 3/plan/docs/01_architecture.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/first big plan/agent_platform_plan_final.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/jido-main/README.md.

ADR-003 Universal Agent Runtime profile hot-swap: ACCEPTED. One long-lived runtime per session. Profile swap changes prompt layers, tool allowlist/policy overlays, default task category, default/pinned model, structured-output expectations, and review/verification posture. Swap preserves Session Memory, Task Memory, Event Log cursor, attachments, compacted context, Artifact Store refs, approvals, MCP handles where safe, and provider cache hints where supported. Swaps occur at safe turn boundaries unless explicitly interrupted/forked. Evidence: Code Puppy base_agent.py, _builder.py, _runtime.py, agent_manager.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/extensions/15_mcp_and_agents.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md.

ADR-004 Model routing/provider strategy: ACCEPTED. Use ModelRouter -> ProviderAdapter -> StructuredOutputLayer. Router owns model registry lookup, provider selection, per-profile pins, retries, fallback, round-robin, streaming normalization, tool-call normalization, telemetry, cost/latency, and capability checks. Start small (Anthropic-compatible, OpenAI-compatible, fast user-preferred compatible provider such as Cerebras if still preferred), then expand. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/model_factory.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/round_robin_model.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/models.json; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/upgrades/11_provider_contract.md.

ADR-005 Tool/MCP/approval/task gates: ACCEPTED. Every tool intent passes policy before execution. Planning/research mode is read-only. Mutating/executing tools require an active task. Patch/write/shell/remote require gates and approvals. Verification commands are allowlisted by name, not arbitrary shell strings. MCP tools are namespaced and default write-classification unless operator explicitly allowlists as read-only. Blocked attempts are persisted as events. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan 3/plan/docs/05_tools_policy_approvals_verification.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/tools/__init__.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/mcp_/manager.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/extensions/15_mcp_and_agents.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/core/src/exec_policy.rs.

ADR-006 Hook system and five invariants: ACCEPTED. Hook system wraps tool calls, task changes, provider events, lifecycle events, and approvals. Deterministic hooks run before LLM steering. Outcomes: continue, block, modify, guide. Persist hook results and blocks to Event Log. Five invariants: (1) no mutating/executing tool without active task; (2) category-based tool gating; (3) priority-ordered hook execution; (4) guidance after task operations; (5) complete event capture. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/swarm-sdk-architecture.pdf; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/first big plan/agent_platform_plan_final.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/code_puppy-main/code_puppy/hook_engine/engine.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/dispatcher.rs.

ADR-007 State/memory/recovery naming and storage: ACCEPTED. Use only practical renamed concepts: Event Log, Session Memory, Task Memory, Compacted Context, Finding Store, Persistent Memory, Project Lessons, Artifact Store, Error Log, Repair Queue, checkpoints. Local-first store by default; memory/test, SQLite/local, and optional Postgres/team adapters. Append-only Event Log is audit/replay source. Artifact Store holds diffs, stdout/stderr, prompt-debug payloads, provider payloads, snapshots, and repair evidence. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md; plan_v0.3 FINAL_ULTIMATE_PLAN.md; upgrades/12_durable_execution.md; Code Puppy session_storage.py; Codex 0006_memories.sql.

ADR-008 SSH remote execution: ACCEPTED. Local machine is control plane; remote hosts are less-trusted SSH execution targets. Requirements: explicit SSH profiles, host-key verification, least-privilege remote credentials, no raw master secrets copied remotely, heartbeat, cancellation, stdout/stderr/artifact streaming, remote sandbox/workdir boundaries, reconnect/resync, result merge through Event Log and Artifact Store. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/first big plan/agent_platform_plan_final.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/exec-server/README.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/oh-my-pi-main/README.md.

ADR-009 Nano Repair Mode: ACCEPTED. Lifecycle: detect internal exceptions/supervisor crashes/compile failures/smoke failures/tool failures/remote failures into Error Log; queue Repair Queue item; pre-turn check open repairs; diagnose under debugging task with diagnostic-only tools; produce repair plan and approvals; patch only under active acting repair task; compile/rebuild; smoke test; hot reload where safe or restart subtree/full runtime; checkpoint/version marker; persist Project Lessons/Persistent Memory; rollback failed repair and keep Error Log open; provide minimal safe fallback repair binary/release that can diagnose -> patch -> compile -> smoke -> hand control back. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/first big plan/agent_platform_plan_final.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/upgrades/12_durable_execution.md.

ADR-010 Safe LLExpert-style command correction: ACCEPTED. Since exact LLExpert source is missing, implement behavior from prompt requirements plus verified adjacent shell-safety docs: suggestions/refinements only; classify risk; explain corrected command; require explicit approval before execution; never auto-run dangerous shell/write/remote commands; block or require approval for nested command substitution unless allowlisted. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/llxprt-code-main/docs/shell-replacement.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/core/src/exec_policy.rs.

ADR-011 Missing-source handling: ACCEPTED. Do not invent missing facts or paths. G-domain/agent-domain and exact LLExpert were not locally verified. Oh My Pi and Pi Mono exist but are DX/packaging inspiration only, not core architecture authority. Evidence: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/llxprt-code-main/README.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/oh-my-pi-main/README.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/pi-mono-main/README.md.

comments.author: pack-leader-0485da
comments.text:
SYNTHESIS PART 3/3 — Downstream guidance, acceptance checklist, risks, verification for tet-d1c

Guidance for tet-sip (Global Vision deliverable): write the end-state architecture as a decision narrative, not a backlog. Required emphasis: Tet/Owl is standalone Elixir/OTP CLI-first; Phoenix is optional adapter only; Universal Agent Runtime is central; profiles hot-swap while preserving Session Memory, Task Memory, Event Log cursor, attachments, compacted context, Artifact Store refs, approvals, and safe handles/cache hints; tools/MCP are policy-gated; hook/task system enforces the five invariants; state uses Event Log, Session Memory, Task Memory, Finding Store, Persistent Memory, Artifact Store, Repair Queue, Project Lessons, Error Log, checkpoints; remote execution uses local control plane plus less-trusted SSH targets; Nano Repair Mode lifecycle is detect -> queue -> pre-turn check -> diagnose -> plan -> patch -> compile -> smoke -> reload/restart -> checkpoint -> learn -> rollback/fallback binary.

Main citation anchors for tet-sip: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/FINAL_ULTIMATE_PLAN.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan 3/plan/docs/01_architecture.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/tet agent_platform_minimal_core.md; Code Puppy base_agent.py/_builder.py/_runtime.py/agent_manager.py/model_factory.py/round_robin_model.py/models.json/tools/__init__.py/mcp_/manager.py/hook_engine/engine.py/session_storage.py/messaging/bus.py/config.py; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/upgrades/11_provider_contract.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/upgrades/12_durable_execution.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/plan claude/plan_v0.3/extensions/15_mcp_and_agents.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/swarm-sdk-architecture.pdf; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/check this plans/first big plan/agent_platform_plan_final.md; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/core/src/exec_policy.rs; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/hooks/src/engine/dispatcher.rs; /Users/adam2/projects/tet/source/codex-main/codex-rs/exec-server/README.md.

Conflict-resolution rules for tet-sip: Code Puppy behavior inspires core agent parity, but Elixir/OTP boundaries decide runtime shape; CLI wins over Phoenix; safety/policy wins over convenience; MCP convenience never bypasses policy; custom OTP wins over Jido until measured pain justifies adoption; old memory terminology must be renamed to approved terms; missing sources cannot be cited.

Guidance for tet-los (Phased Implementation deliverable): convert ADRs into safe increments. Use prompt.md mandatory Phase 0-20 order unless explicitly justified. Dependency sequencing: do not build Phoenix before CLI/runtime/store contracts; do not build MCP before tool policy/approval gates; do not build remote execution before local shell/tool policy; do not build Nano Repair patching before Error Log, Artifact Store, approvals, verifier commands, checkpoints; do not build Persistent Memory before Event Log and Finding Store; do not adopt Jido before custom OTP seams and measured pain exist.

Suggested phase anchors for tet-los: Phase 0 research/ADR uses prompt.md and prior plans; Phase 1 standalone CLI MVP uses tet agent_platform_minimal_core.md and plan 3 architecture; Phase 3 prompt/session uses Code Puppy BaseAgent/builder/runtime/history/compaction; Phase 4 model router uses model_factory.py, round_robin_model.py, models.json, upgrades/11_provider_contract.md; Phase 5 Universal Agent Runtime uses Code Puppy agent manager and extensions/15_mcp_and_agents.md; Phase 6 read-only tools uses tools/__init__.py; Phase 7 hooks/tasks uses swarm-sdk-architecture.pdf, first big plan, hook_engine/engine.py, Codex dispatcher; Phase 8 mutating tools uses plan 3 tools/policy and Codex exec_policy.rs; Phase 10 memory/checkpoints uses durable execution, session_storage.py, Codex state migration; Phase 12 MCP uses mcp_/manager.py; Phase 16 remote uses first big plan and Codex exec-server; Phase 17 repair uses prompt.md, first big plan, durable execution; Phase 20 packaging/DX can cite Oh My Pi/Pi Mono/LLxprt as later inspiration.

Acceptance checklist for closing tet-d1c:
- [x] Investigation plan and synthesis table produced and persisted in bd comment.
- [x] ADR-style decisions produced and persisted for CLI/Phoenix boundary, OTP/Jido, Universal Agent Runtime hot-swap, model routing, tools/MCP/task gates, hook system/five invariants, state naming/storage, SSH remote execution, Nano Repair Mode, safe command correction, missing-source handling.
- [x] Downstream guidance for tet-sip and tet-los produced and persisted.
- [x] No implementation code generated.
- [x] No final plan deliverable files created by tet-d1c.
- [x] Renamed memory/state terms used as final concepts.
- [x] CLI standalone / Phoenix optional boundary maintained.
- [x] Universal Agent Runtime and Nano Repair Mode requirements maintained.
- [x] Missing source families handled without invented paths.

Risks / open questions:
1. G-domain/agent-domain and exact LLExpert sources were not locally verified; downstream must not cite unavailable internals.
2. Jido adoption should wait for measured custom OTP pain.
3. Provider adapters/tool-call normalization can sprawl; keep first provider set small.
4. MCP trust classification must default safe even if annoying.
5. Profile swaps during streams/tools/approvals need strict turn-boundary rules.
6. Nano Repair fallback binary must remain independent enough to run when main CLI is broken.
7. Remote execution credential scoping/redaction needs early tests.
8. Event Log retention/compaction policy must prevent unbounded growth while preserving auditability.

Verification:
- Command reported by executor: git -C /Users/adam2/projects/tet-d1c status --short -> M  .beads/issues.jsonl.
- Pack QA independently verified final deliverables absent in worktree: /Users/adam2/projects/tet-d1c/plans/ELIXIR_CLI_AGENT_GLOBAL_VISION.md absent; /Users/adam2/projects/tet-d1c/plans/ELIXIR_CLI_AGENT_PHASED_IMPLEMENTATION.md absent.
- Pack QA independently verified cited local source paths exist in /Users/adam2/projects/tet and no implementation/source files were generated in /Users/adam2/projects/tet-d1c.

comments.author: asx8678
comments.text: Investigation complete. Extracted notes via code-puppy covering: agent system (BaseAgent + _builder/_runtime/_compaction/_history pattern), 22 specialized agents catalog, model factory + provider layer (15 model types, round-robin, models.json), tool layer (~65 tools across files/shell/browser/MCP), MCP integration (manager/registry/health/circuit-breaker/error-isolation), hook engine (Claude Code-compatible, exit-code semantics 0/1/2), pickle-based session storage, MessageBus dual-queue pub/sub, ~20 plugins, XDG-compliant config. Read prior plans: tet agent_platform_minimal_core.md (3-mode model, allowlisted verifier), plan_v0.3 (Tet umbrella + durable execution + provider contract + bidirectional tool-call mapping + multi-agent profiles), first big plan (dual-surface architecture with hook system + 5 deterministic invariants). Codex CLI inspection deferred — patterns already covered by existing plans. Swarm SDK PDF: 5 invariants are explicitly listed in prompt.md §5.4 + first_big_plan §4.5; PDF binary content not text-extractable in current env, noted as missing-but-conceptually-covered. **Missing sources** (per prompt §2): G-domain, LLExpert, Oh My Pi, Pi Mono — referenced but not present in ; will be noted in deliverables.

title: Elixir CLI Agent Master Planning (Owl CLI)
description:
Research-and-planning effort to synthesize Code Puppy, Codex CLI, Swarm SDK, and prior planning materials into a master plan for an Elixir/OTP-native, CLI-first AI coding agent.

Source meta-prompt: /Users/adam2/projects/tet/prompt.md

DELIVERABLES (the only outputs allowed):
1. plans/ELIXIR_CLI_AGENT_GLOBAL_VISION.md — end-state architecture (27 required sections, Mermaid topology diagram, hook registry, 5 invariants, glossary)
2. plans/ELIXIR_CLI_AGENT_PHASED_IMPLEMENTATION.md — 21 phases (Phase 0 → Phase 20) using the strict phase template, plus a BD-ready backlog

HARD RULES (from prompt.md §0):
- No implementation code, patches, or source files generated
- Read-only investigation only
- Use renamed memory terms (Event Log, Session Memory, Task Memory, Finding Store, Persistent Memory, Artifact Store, Repair Queue, Project Lessons) — NOT Bronze/Silver/Gold
- CLI is core, Phoenix LiveView is optional adapter only
- Universal Agent Runtime with profile hot-swap
- Nano Repair Mode for self-healing
- All important claims cite local source paths (no invented paths)

ACCEPTANCE CHECKLIST (prompt.md §12):
- [ ] Both required Markdown files exist
- [ ] No implementation code generated
- [ ] Code Puppy parity is explicit and traceable
- [ ] CLI is standalone; Phoenix is structurally optional
- [ ] Universal Agent Runtime profile hot-swap fully specified
- [ ] Task system, hook system, plan mode, steering, 5 invariants fully specified
- [ ] Memory terms use renamed names
- [ ] Nano Repair Mode fully specified (capture/diagnose/patch/compile/smoke/reload/rollback/fallback-binary)
- [ ] SSH remote execution fully specified
- [ ] LLExpert-style command correction safe-by-default
- [ ] Roadmap starts with minimal CLI and grows safely
- [ ] Every phase uses required template + BD/Beads decomposition hints
- [ ] BD-ready backlog included at the end
- [ ] Important claims cite actual local source paths

comments.author: code-puppy-fd0da3
comments.text:
code-puppy-fd0da3 analysis note:

Analyzed prompt.md as an execution spec, not as an instruction to immediately produce the two final plan files in this turn. The BD epic tet-db6 is the correct planning container for executing the prompt safely.

Execution plan captured by this epic:
